title: Sphaerostephanos irayensis
intro: Sphaerostephanos irayensis is a species of fern, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various conditions such as arthritis and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of Sphaerostephanos irayensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas, capsules, and extracts to harness its bioactive compounds.
taxonomy: Sphaerostephanos irayensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is native to the Philippines, specifically in the island of Luzon. It belongs to the genus Sphaerostephanos, which is a group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical regions. The classification of Sphaerostephanos irayensis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum and class to which it belongs are Pteridophyta and Polypodiopsida, respectively. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of this plant.
